repo_prior_art.cite: doc/API.md Section 3.1 ManskiNonparametricBounds_Study: bounded-outcome Manski-style treatment-effect bounds

published_prior_art.cite: Manski1990NonparametricBounds and SusmannMcCleanDiaz2025NonOverlapATEBounds: bounded missing-outcome cells contribute worst-case width proportional to missing-cell mass
KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification and HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity cover tail-rate estimation/inference; Manski1990NonparametricBounds gives the missing-mass width baseline

HongLeungLi2020FinitePopulationLimitedOverlap and Rothe2017LimitedOverlapCI cover limited-overlap inference/rates, not sharp one-tail width; the one-tail claim follows from the same bounded-completion formula

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The bounded-outcome missing-arm mass formula is already the Manski-style worst-case completion kernel; the proposal itself calls this theorem routine.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: tier=field below novelty_target=flagship; current kernel is a regular-variation re-expression of bounded missing-mass width, not a strict comparator-anchored frontier.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: Flagship comparison cites KhanTamer2010IrregularIdentification, HeilerKazak2021ExtremePropensity, and SusmannMcCleanDiaz2025NonOverlapATEBounds without location-level anchors for the specific phase-law gap.

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: Consumer is stated generically as treated-tail sensitivity analysis for ATT studies, with no named downstream paper, open question, or applied domain.

soundness_flags.label: Theorem 1
soundness_flags.one_line: The ATE formula needs either epsilon < 1/2 or an intersection residual: on T_epsilon^0 cap T_epsilon^1 both potential-outcome means can vary, giving width 4B on the intersection rather than 2B.

soundness_flags.label: Sanity-check corollaries
soundness_flags.one_line: The Manski corner says full no-information treatment-effect width is 2B, but with Y(0),Y(1) in [-B,B] the treatment-effect range has width 4B.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 1
soundness_flags.one_line: Once Theorem 1 and regular variation are assumed, A_epsilon asymp epsilon^(min alpha - 1) is essentially a definition unfold plus tail-mass asymptotics, below the three-step flagship floor.

soundness_flags.label: Conjecture 2
soundness_flags.one_line: The one-tail ATT phase follows by changing measure to P_{X|D=1} and applying the same tail-mass asymptotic, so the current proof shape is not flagship-grade.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Patch the width formula by adding the epsilon < 1/2 disjoint-tail condition or the extra intersection term, and correct the Manski corner to width 4B.

Upgrade Conjecture 1 into a strict comparator theorem against Lee-Weidner or Susmann-McClean-Diaz: construct two laws with identical comparator inputs but different alpha-profile widths, with a location-anchored citation to the exact comparator functional.
Replace the generic consumer clauses with named downstream use cases and add location-level anchors for Khan-Tamer, Heiler-Kazak, Hong-Leung-Li, Rothe, and Susmann-McClean-Diaz.
Correct the Heiler-Kazak bibliography metadata to Journal of Econometrics 222(2), 1083-1108, while preserving the stable bibkey.

recommended_next_step: Revise locally: fix the width/corner algebra first, then either downgrade the tier ambition to field or add a strict published-comparator frontier that makes the alpha profile do more than reindex Manski mass width.
